title: mentha × rotundifolia subsp. velutina
intro: Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. velutina, also known as woolly mint, is a subspecies of mint plant characterized by its distinctive velvety leaves. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties of being an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of woolly mint include relief from digestive problems, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. Woolly mint can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being added to skincare products due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
